framework.name: Visa TAP Registry-Based Verification Model
framework.description: Visa's Trusted Agent Protocol uses a public key registry architecture with RFC 9421 cryptographic signatures that are domain-bound, operation-specific, and timestamp-validated. Each agent request includes signature components (timestamp, session ID, key ID, algorithm) verified against a central Agent Registry, with CDN-level proxy interception preventing unsigned traffic from reaching merchant backends.
framework.raw_quote: The agent presents a secure signature that includes timestamps, a unique session identifier, key identifier, and algorithm identifier, allowing you to verify that the signature is current and prevent relays or replays... Every request from a trusted agent is cryptographically locked to a merchant's specific website and the exact page with which the agent is interacting.

source.title: GitHub - visa/trusted-agent-protocol: Establishing a universal standard of trust between AI agents and merchants for the next phase of agentic commerce. · GitHub
